# Cosign

> Cosign co-signs a Gnosis Safe transaction off-chain, coordinated over MsgBoard (a
> proof-of-work-stamped, ephemeral message board with no trusted server). Owners sign locally with
> their own wallet, post a small ABI-encoded `SignatureRecord` share to the board under a rotating
> daily category key, and any participant reads the shares back, verifies each one against the
> Safe's own live owner set and threshold, aggregates them into an `execTransaction` `signatures`
> blob, and executes once quorum is met. A Safe's on-chain `checkNSignatures` is the actual security
> boundary; cosign's own verification is a UI convenience, not a trust requirement.

## Docs

- [Human-readable docs](https://cosign.msgboard.xyz/docs.html): full walkthrough of the trust model,
  the step-by-step co-sign flow, the Create-a-Safe (v1.4.1) flow, and the architecture.
- [App](https://cosign.msgboard.xyz/): the live wizard UI (React + Vite, `packages/cosign-web`).

## SDK — `@msgboard/cosign`

Framework-agnostic TypeScript package (`packages/cosign/src`). Zero chain writes; pure board +
crypto. Key entry points (all exported from the package root):

- `postSignature(board, { namespace, scope, record, now? })` — encodes a `SignatureRecord` and
  posts it to the board under `currentKey(namespace, scope, now)`.
- `readSignatures(board, { namespace, scope, days, now? })` — sweeps the rolling window of category
  keys, decodes every board entry, skips undecodable junk, dedupes by `keccak256` of the raw bytes.
- `groupByDigest(records)` — buckets records by the digest (`safeTxHash`) they sign.
- `aggregate(records, adapter)` — keeps only records `adapter.verify()` accepts, then returns
  `adapter.order()`'s output as `{ signer, signature }` pairs. Verification failures are excluded,
  not trusted.
- `makeSafeAdapter({ publicClient, safe, chainId })` — the Gnosis Safe `CosignAdapter`: verifies an
  EOA signature by recovering over the digest and checking current Safe ownership; verifies
  EIP-1271 contract-owner signatures against the owner's `isValidSignature`; orders shares strictly
  ascending by signer (the order Safe's `checkNSignatures`/blob format requires).
  Also available: `makeSafe4337Adapter` (Safe4337Module UserOp signing) and
  `makeRhinestoneOwnableAdapter` (Rhinestone ownable-validator UserOps).
- `safeTransactionDigest(safeTx, chainId, safe)` — the EIP-712 SafeTx digest; byte-equal to the
  Safe's on-chain `getTransactionHash(...)`. No `EIP712Domain` name/version — only chainId +
  verifyingContract, matching Safe's own `domainSeparator()`.
- `buildSignatureBlob(orderedRecords)` / `buildExecTransactionArgs(orderedRecords, safeTx)` — build
  the final Safe `signatures` bytes / the full `execTransaction(...)` positional argument tuple.
- `recoverEffectiveSigner(record)` — digest-agnostic signer recovery: `ecrecover` over the digest for
  EIP-712, `personal_sign`-prefixed recovery for ECDSA/`eth_sign`, or the claimed contract owner for
  EIP-1271.
- `encodeSafeMeta(safeTx, safe, chainId)` / `decodeSafeMeta(meta)` — pack/unpack the full SafeTx
  tuple into `SignatureRecord.meta` so any reader can reconstruct `execTransaction`'s arguments from
  the posted shares alone.
- `SAFE_V141` deploy constants + `predictSafeAddress`/`confirmDeploy` live in
  `packages/cosign-web/src/lib/deploy-safe.ts` (app-side, not yet re-exported from the SDK root).

## Endpoints

- **cosign-relay** (gasless Safe deploy, optional) — `https://cosign.msgboard.xyz/relay`. See
  "Gasless Safe deploy" below for the request shape. `GET /relay/health` → `{ "ok": true }`.
  `GET /relay/config` → `{ "chains": [943], "powBits": 20, "sponsors": [{ "chainId", "address",
  "balance" }] }` — which chains currently have a funded relay key (only request-deploy on a chain
  this lists), the PoW difficulty to solve, and each sponsor wallet's address + native balance
  (wei, decimal string) so anyone can top it up. Currently sponsors chain 943 only; 369 activates
  once `RELAY_KEY_369` is funded; chain 1 (Ethereum) is never sponsored.
- **safe-indexer** — `GET https://cosign.msgboard.xyz/safe-indexer/owners/:address/safes?chainId=<369|943>`
  → `{ "safes": ["0x…", …] }` (checksummed, deduped; same response shape as the official Safe
  Transaction Service). Self-hosted (Ponder-indexed) because PulseChain has no official Safe Tx
  Service. Ethereum mainnet (chain 1) uses the real upstream instead:
  `GET https://safe-transaction-mainnet.safe.global/api/v1/owners/:address/safes/`.
  Read-only discovery convenience — never trusted; owners/threshold are always re-read from the Safe
  contract itself.
- **cosign-archive** — `GET https://cosign-archive.msgboard.xyz/cosign/:namespace/:scope/signatures?days=<N>`
  → `{ "signatures": [{ digest, signer, signature, scheme, meta }, …] }`. Serves decoded shares that
  have scrolled off the live board's window so they still count toward quorum. The client unions
  board ∪ archive (board wins on collision) and degrades to board-only if this is unreachable.

## Category-key formula

```
categoryKey(namespace, scope, isoDate) = keccak256(utf8Bytes(`${namespace}:${scope}:${isoDate}`))

namespace = "cosign"
scope     = `safe:${chainId}:${safeAddress.toLowerCase()}`
isoDate   = UTC YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. "2026-07-02")
```

A reader sweeps today's key plus the prior `days - 1` UTC days (cosign-web's app uses `days = 7`).

## Record shape (`SignatureRecord` / `RECORD_ABI`)

ABI tuple, order is law — `(bytes32 digest, address signer, bytes signature, uint8 scheme, bytes meta)`:

```
digest     bytes32   the signed digest (e.g. a Safe safeTxHash)
signer     address   the claimed signer
signature  bytes     e.g. 65-byte r||s||v for ECDSA/EIP-712
scheme     uint8      0 = ECDSA (eth_sign), 1 = EIP1271, 2 = EIP712
meta       bytes     scheme-specific payload; for a Safe EIP-712 record this ABI-encodes
                     (to, value, data, operation, safeTxGas, baseGas, gasPrice, gasToken,
                      refundReceiver, nonce, safe, chainId) — `0x` when unused
```

Encode/decode with `encodeRecord`/`decodeRecord` from `@msgboard/cosign`.

## Supported chains

Ethereum mainnet (1), PulseChain (369), PulseChain v4 testnet (943). The MsgBoard the shares travel
over is independent of the chain the Safe lives on — it only needs the `msgboard_` RPC module.

## Gasless Safe deploy (optional, via cosign-relay)

An alternative to Step 2 of Create-a-Safe (v1.4.1): a caller with no native gas can have
`cosign-relay` submit `createProxyWithNonce` and pay gas from its own funded key, for a **plain
owners+threshold multisig setup only** (`to == 0x0`, `data == 0x`, canonical `fallbackHandler`,
`paymentToken/payment/paymentReceiver` all zero/empty — anything else is rejected before any other
check runs). Steps 1/3/4 of Create-a-Safe (predict, verify mined==predicted, hand off) are
unchanged.

Request digest — MUST match byte-for-byte, this binds the request to one exact chain/singleton/
Safe-config so it can never be replayed elsewhere:

```
digest = keccak256(abi.encode(
  ['uint256', 'address', 'bytes32', 'uint256'],
  [chainId, singleton, keccak256(initializer), saltNonce],
))
```

Two gates the request must pass, in addition to the plain-multisig check above:

1. **PoW** — find `powNonce` (32 bytes) such that `keccak256(digest ++ pad(powNonce, 32))`, as a
   uint256, is `< 2^256 / 2^powBits` (`powBits` from `GET /relay/config`, default 20). Walletless
   CPU cost, no funds required. Grind off the main thread if in a browser.
2. **Signed owner** — an EIP-191 personal-sign of the raw digest
   (`recoverMessageAddress({ message: { raw: digest } })`) by one of the setup's own `owners[]`.
   Not an EIP-712 `SafeTx` signature — this is a different, deploy-time-only digest.

`POST https://cosign.msgboard.xyz/relay/deploy-safe`:

```json
{
  "chainId": 943,
  "singleton": "0x29fcB43b46531BcA003ddC8FCB67FFE91900C762",
  "initializer": "0x...",
  "saltNonce": "12345",
  "signature": "0x...",
  "powNonce": "0x..."
}
```

→ `200 { "txHash": "0x...", "proxy": "0x..." }` (still run `confirmDeploy` against this txHash —
CREATE2 is submitter-independent, so the relay can never substitute a different address than the
one predicted and signed for), `400 { "error": "..." }` for any gate failure, `429` for the relay's
per-IP daily rate limit (default 5/day).
